title: Cache Identity, TTL, Clock Injection, Readonly Copies, and Capacity · Inference Brief: Pokedex Ts Cache Key
summary: The cache keys raw input, reads Date.now internally, shares mutable objects, conflates stale with absent, stores aborted promises, and has no entry or byte ceiling. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts cache key increment in observable bounded TypeScript resource cache. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, ch…
objectives: Create a canonical typed key from endpoint, resolved identity, page parameters, and representation rather than user spelling.
Generate command sequences and verify cursor, identity, ordering, bounds, stale-result exclusion, and state after every transition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pokedex Ts Cache Key
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 4333 during an offline mobile-library catalog migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile canonical TTL cache decisions, runtime ownership, error policy, and capacity eviction; each Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, and handle needs explicit ownership; then inject one redirect, prototype-like key, terminal control, path, file, or signal failure and reject it safely. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Create a canonical typed key from endpoint, resolved identity, page parameters, and representation rather than user spelling.
